wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the Invoicing Rails application. Include detailed installation instructions, environment setup, database configuration, and initial project setup. Document the development workflow, common commands, and first steps to get the application running. Provide step-by-step guidance for new developers to set up their local development environment, configure dependencies, and run the application successfully. Include troubleshooting tips for common setup issues and verification steps to ensure proper installation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Devise Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the authentication system implementation using Devise. Document the User model configuration, Devise modules setup, and authentication flow. Explain session management, password policies, and security configurations. Include examples of customizing authentication views, adding custom validation rules, and implementing multi-factor authentication. Address common authentication issues, performance optimization, and security best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Design & Architecture Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the system design and architecture patterns of the Invoicing Rails application. Describe the MVC architecture implementation, service layer patterns, and component organization. Document the separation of concerns between controllers, models, views, and JavaScript controllers. Explain the use of concerns for shared functionality, service objects for business logic, and the overall architectural decisions. Include diagrams showing how different layers interact and communicate. Address design patterns like repository pattern, observer pattern, and strategy pattern where applicable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Invoice Lifecycle Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for invoice lifecycle management. Explain the complete invoice workflow from creation through payment tracking, including status transitions (draft, sent, paid, overdue). Document the Invoice model's business logic, validation rules, and state management. Cover invoice creation process, editing capabilities, deletion handling, and archival strategies. Include examples of creating new invoices, updating existing ones, and managing invoice statuses. Address invoice numbering, date handling, currency formatting, and integration with client records.

wiki_catalogs.name: Clients API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Clients RESTful endpoints. Document HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), URL patterns (/api/clients), request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Include detailed examples of client listing with pagination, individual client retrieval, creation with validation, updates, and deletion operations. Specify request parameters including search filters, sorting options, and pagination controls. Document response formats with JBuilder templates, error codes, and status messages. Address client data structure, relationships with invoices, and business rules validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Stimulus Controllers
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for Stimulus.js controllers architecture. Explain the controller registration system in index.js, data attribute patterns, and lifecycle methods. Document each controller's purpose: form_validation_controller for client-side validation, charts_controller for Chart.js integration, modal_controller for dialog management, filter_controller for search functionality, and specialized controllers like clientselect_controller and itemselect_controller for dropdown interactions. Include examples of creating new controllers, handling DOM events, managing state, and communicating with Turbo Streams. Address controller composition patterns, performance optimization, and debugging techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Invoicing Rails application. Describe the high-level system design, MVC architecture patterns, and component interactions. Document the technology stack including Ruby on Rails, Stimulus.js, Turbo Streams, Chart.js, and Tailwind CSS. Explain the directory structure, data flow architecture, and integration patterns. Include system context diagrams showing how controllers, models, views, and JavaScript components interact. Address cross-cutting concerns like authentication, internationalization, and real-time updates. Document the deployment topology and infrastructure requirements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Registration & Onboarding Flow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the user registration and onboarding workflow. Detail the multi-step registration process, freelancer vs company account selection, and profile completion flows. Document the AfterRegisterController logic, form validations, and data persistence. Include examples of customizing registration steps, adding new account types, and implementing conditional form fields. Address error handling, progress tracking, and user experience optimization during onboarding.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack & Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the technology stack and dependencies used in the Invoicing Rails application. Document all major gems including Devise for authentication, Turbo for real-time updates, Stimulus.js for frontend interactivity, Chart.js for data visualization, and Tailwind CSS for styling. Explain the role of each dependency, version compatibility requirements, and integration points. Include information about build tools, asset pipeline configuration, and deployment dependencies. Address alternative technologies and migration paths where relevant.

wiki_catalogs.name: Line Item Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for line item management within invoices. Detail the LineItem model structure, associations with Invoice and Item models, and calculation logic. Explain how line items are created, updated, and deleted both individually and in bulk. Document the nested form implementation for adding multiple line items to an invoice. Cover quantity management, unit pricing, discount application, and tax calculations per line item. Include examples of dynamic line item addition/removal, real-time price updates, and validation rules for line item data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Invoices API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Invoices RESTful endpoints. Document HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), URL patterns (/api/invoices), request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Include detailed examples of invoice listing with filtering by status/date ranges, individual invoice retrieval with line items, creation with nested line item data, updates, and deletion operations. Specify request parameters including status filters, date ranges, client associations, and tax calculations. Document response formats with complete invoice structure including line items, totals, and calculated fields. Address invoice lifecycle states, validation rules, and financial calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Turbo Streams Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Turbo Streams real-time updates implementation. Explain how partial page updates work without full page reloads, including stream actions (append, prepend, replace, remove) and target element selection. Document each turbo stream template: clients create/show/update streams for dynamic client management, invoices update stream for real-time invoice modifications, items show stream for catalog updates, and countries regions stream for cascading dropdowns. Include examples of creating custom turbo stream responses, handling stream errors, and optimizing performance. Address browser compatibility, fallback strategies, and debugging turbo stream interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the user management system. Detail the authentication implementation using Devise, user registration flows, profile management, and multi-language support. Document the User and UserProfile models, their relationships, and validation rules. Explain the after-registration workflow, freelancer vs company account types, and user preferences. Include examples of customizing authentication flows, extending user profiles, and implementing role-based access control. Address security considerations, password policies, and session management.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Profile & Preferences
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for user profile management and preference settings. Document the UserProfile model structure, relationships with User model, and data validation rules. Explain address management, personal information handling, and preference storage. Include examples of extending user profiles, adding custom fields, and implementing profile completion tracking. Address data privacy considerations, profile photo uploads, and internationalization support for user data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow & Request Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the data flow and request processing architecture in the Invoicing Rails application. Document the complete HTTP request lifecycle from route handling through controller actions, model validation, database operations, and view rendering. Explain Turbo Streams integration for real-time partial updates, Stimulus.js controller interactions, and client-server communication patterns. Include sequence diagrams showing typical user workflows like invoice creation, client management, and dashboard updates. Address error handling, validation flows, and asynchronous operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tax Calculation Engine
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the tax calculation system. Explain how taxes are calculated at both line item and invoice levels, including IVA/VAT handling, percentage-based taxes, and fixed amount taxes. Document the tax calculation formulas, rounding rules, and currency formatting. Cover multi-tax scenarios, tax-exempt items, and regional tax variations. Include examples of different tax configurations, custom tax rates, and tax reporting features. Address tax validation, error handling, and compliance considerations for different jurisdictions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Items API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Items RESTful endpoints. Document HTTP methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE), URL patterns (/api/items), request/response schemas, and authentication requirements. Include detailed examples of item catalog listing with search and filtering, individual item retrieval, creation with pricing and tax configuration, updates, and deletion operations. Specify request parameters including category filters, price ranges, availability status, and search queries. Document response formats with item details, pricing information, tax settings, and usage statistics. Address item catalog management, pricing strategies, and integration with invoice line items.

wiki_catalogs.name: Form Handling & Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for form handling and validation strategies. Explain the form_validation_controller implementation for client-side validation, SimpleForm configuration for enhanced form rendering, and server-side validation integration. Document form patterns used across clients, invoices, and items forms, including nested attributes, file uploads, and dynamic field generation. Include examples of custom validators, error display patterns, and form submission handling with Turbo Streams. Address accessibility considerations, mobile form optimization, and cross-browser compatibility issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Client Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the client management system. Thoroughly explain the Client model implementation, CRUD operations, search and filtering capabilities, and client relationship tracking. Document the controller actions, view partials, and helper methods. Include examples of client creation, editing, deletion, and advanced search functionality. Explain the Turbo Stream integration for real-time updates, form validation, and error handling. Address client history tracking, status management, and integration with invoices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Multi-language & Localization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the multi-language support implementation. Document locale detection, translation key organization, and dynamic language switching. Explain route translation, form label localization, and error message internationalization. Include examples of adding new languages, managing translation files, and implementing context-specific translations. Address performance considerations for large translation sets and testing strategies for localized content.

wiki_catalogs.name: Directory Structure & Organization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the directory structure and file organization of the Invoicing Rails application. Explain the purpose and contents of each major directory including app/, config/, db/, test/, and vendor/. Document the organization patterns within each directory such as models, controllers, views, JavaScript controllers, and initializers. Include guidelines for adding new features, organizing related files, and maintaining consistency. Address naming conventions, file placement rules, and best practices for code organization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Real-time Invoice Updates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document the real-time update system for invoices using Turbo Streams and Stimulus.js controllers. Explain how partial page updates work when modifying line items, quantities, or prices. Detail the recalculation controller that handles automatic total computations and the form validation controller for client-side validation. Cover Turbo Stream templates for invoice updates, event handling patterns, and state synchronization between server and client. Include examples of implementing custom real-time features, debugging Turbo Stream issues, and optimizing performance for large invoices with many line items.

wiki_catalogs.name: Turbo Streams API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for Turbo Stream real-time communication endpoints. Document Turbo Stream message formats, target element selectors, and action types (append, replace, remove). Include examples of real-time client updates, invoice status changes, and item catalog modifications. Specify stream names, HTML partial structures, and JavaScript event handling patterns. Document WebSocket connection management, error recovery strategies, and performance optimization techniques. Address real-time form submissions, live validation feedback, and progressive enhancement patterns. Include debugging tools and monitoring approaches for Turbo Stream connections.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components & Interactions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for reusable UI components and interaction patterns. Document the modal_controller for dialog management, menu_controller for navigation, and shared components like modals, menus, sidebars, and navigation bars. Explain responsive design patterns, mobile-first approaches, and accessibility compliance. Include examples of component composition, event delegation, and state management. Address CSS framework integration with Tailwind CSS, animation patterns, and performance optimization for complex interactions. Document component APIs, customization options, and integration guidelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Invoice Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the invoice management system. Detail the Invoice and LineItem models, their associations, and business logic for calculations. Document the complete invoice lifecycle from creation to payment tracking. Explain line item management, tax calculations, total computations, and status workflows. Include examples of creating invoices with multiple line items, updating totals dynamically, and handling different tax scenarios. Address the nested form implementation, real-time calculations, and invoice printing/exporting capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: Item Catalog System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the item catalog system. Thoroughly explain the Item model implementation, product/service management, pricing configuration, and tax settings. Document the CRUD operations, search functionality, and integration with invoices. Include examples of creating items, managing prices and taxes, and reusing items across multiple invoices. Explain the Turbo Stream integration for real-time updates and the relationship between items and line items.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics & Reporting
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the analytics and reporting system. Detail the dashboard implementation, chart visualizations using Chart.js, and financial metrics calculation. Document the Dashboard model, Charts service objects, and JavaScript controllers for interactive charts. Explain paid vs unpaid invoice tracking, revenue analysis, and performance metrics. Include examples of adding new chart types, customizing dashboards, and exporting reports. Address data aggregation strategies, caching mechanisms, and performance optimization for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the frontend architecture. Describe the Stimulus.js controller organization, Turbo Streams implementation, and progressive enhancement patterns. Document each JavaScript controller's purpose, events, and data attributes. Explain form validation strategies, modal dialog management, chart initialization, and filter functionality. Include examples of creating new Stimulus controllers, handling user interactions, and integrating with server-side responses. Address responsive design patterns, accessibility considerations, and performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Internationalization (i18n)
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the internationalization system. Thoroughly explain the locale management, translation key organization, and dynamic language switching. Document the route translator implementation, locale-specific routes, and fallback mechanisms. Include examples of adding new languages, organizing translation files, and handling pluralization. Explain the integration with Devise translations, form errors, and user preferences. Address best practices for maintaining translations and testing multi-language functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the RESTful endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for all JSON APIs. Include examples of client management endpoints, invoice operations, and item catalog APIs. Specify request parameters, response formats, error codes, and status messages. Document Turbo Stream endpoints for real-time updates and WebSocket communication patterns. Address rate limiting, versioning strategies, and API security considerations. Include client implementation guidelines and common use cases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the testing strategy and implementation. Detail the unit testing approach for models, integration testing for controllers, and system testing with Capybara. Document test fixtures, factories, and test data management. Include examples of writing tests for business logic, API endpoints, and user interactions. Explain the testing setup, continuous integration configuration, and code coverage requirements. Address best practices for test organization, mocking external services, and performance testing considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & DevOps
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for deployment and DevOps practices. Detail the production environment configuration, process management with Puma, and asset compilation. Document the deployment pipeline, environment variables, and database migration strategies. Include examples of configuring cloud storage, email services, and monitoring tools. Explain scaling considerations, performance optimization, and backup procedures. Address security hardening, logging strategies, and disaster recovery planning. Document the Render.com deployment configuration and custom build processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & FAQ
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive troubleshooting documentation covering common issues, solutions, and frequently asked questions. Document debugging techniques, log analysis, and performance profiling. Include solutions for database connection issues, asset compilation problems, and deployment failures. Address memory leaks, slow queries, and scalability bottlenecks. Provide diagnostic tools, monitoring approaches, and alerting strategies. Include migration guides for upgrading versions, breaking changes, and compatibility issues. Document known limitations and workarounds for edge cases.

wiki_overview.content:
</think>

<blog>

# Comprehensive Project Analysis: Invoicing Rails Application

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
This is a comprehensive invoicing and billing management application built with Ruby on Rails, designed to help freelancers and companies manage their clients, invoices, items, and financial tracking. The application provides a complete solution for invoice creation, client management, and financial reporting.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Invoice Management**: Create, edit, track, and manage professional invoices
- **Client Administration**: Maintain detailed client information and relationships  
- **Item Catalog**: Manage products/services with pricing and tax configurations
- **Financial Analytics**: Generate charts and reports for business insights
- **Multi-language Support**: Internationalization with English and Spanish locales
- **User Authentication**: Secure user registration and profile management

### Target Audience
- Freelancers and independent contractors
- Small businesses and startups
- Consultants and service providers
- Anyone needing professional invoicing capabilities

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The application follows Rails MVC architecture with modern enhancements:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Frontend Layer] --> B[API Controllers]
    B --> C[Business Logic Models]
    C --> D[Data Access Layer]
    D --> E[(PostgreSQL Database)]
    
    F[JavaScript Controllers] --> G[Turbo Streams]
    G --> H[Partial Updates]
    
    I[Authentication] --> J[Devise]
    K[Charts & Analytics] --> L[Chart.js Integration]
    M[File Storage] --> N[Active Storage]
```

### Design Patterns
- **MVC Architecture**: Standard Rails separation of concerns
- **Concerns Pattern**: Shared functionality extracted into reusable modules
- **Service Objects**: Business logic encapsulation in models
- **Turbo Streams**: Real-time partial updates without full page reloads
- **JBuilder**: JSON API responses for frontend consumption
- **Stimulus.js**: Progressive JavaScript enhancement layer

### System Relationships
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant C as Controller
    participant M as Model
    participant V as View
    
    U->>+C: Create Invoice
    C->>+M: Validate & Save
    M->>M: Calculate Totals
    M-->>-C: Return Invoice
    C->>+V: Render Response
    V-->>-U: Display Invoice
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Bootstrap**: `config/application.rb` - Rails application configuration
- **Route Configuration**: `config/routes.rb` - RESTful routing setup
- **Database Schema**: `db/schema.rb` - Complete database structure definition
- **Gem Dependencies**: `Gemfile` - Ruby gem dependencies and versions

### Core Modules
- **User Management**: `app/models/user.rb`, `app/models/user_profile.rb` - Authentication and user profiles
- **Invoice System**: `app/models/invoice.rb`, `app/models/line_item.rb` - Invoice creation and line item management
- **Client Management**: `app/models/client.rb` - Client data and relationships
- **Item Catalog**: `app/models/item.rb` - Products/services catalog
- **Dashboard Analytics**: `app/models/dashboard.rb` - Business intelligence and reporting

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Config**: `config/environments/` - Development, production, test settings
- **Initializers**: `config/initializers/` - Devise, SimpleForm, Turbo, Chart.js configuration
- **Internationalization**: `config/locales/` - Multi-language support (English/Spanish)
- **Asset Pipeline**: `config/importmap.rb`, `config/tailwind.config.js` - Modern asset management

### External Dependencies
Key gems include:
- **Authentication**: Devise for user management
- **UI Framework**: Tailwind CSS for styling
- **JavaScript**: Stimulus.js controllers and Turbo for interactivity
- **Charts**: Chart.js integration for analytics
- **Forms**: SimpleForm for enhanced form handling
- **Pagination**: Pagy for efficient data pagination
- **Storage**: Active Storage for file uploads

### Integration Points
- **Payment Processing**: Previous payment system integration (migrated)
- **Email Services**: Action Mailer for notifications
- **File Storage**: Cloud storage via Active Storage
- **Chart Rendering**: Client-side chart generation with Chart.js

### Component Relationships
```mermaid
classDiagram
    User <|-- UserProfile
    User ||--o{ Invoice : creates
    User ||--o{ Item : owns
    User ||--o{ Client : manages
    
    Invoice ||--o{ LineItem : contains
    Client ||--o{ Invoice : receives
    Item ||--o{ LineItem : referenced by
    
    Dashboard --> Invoice : analyzes
    Dashboard --> Client : tracks
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Complete Invoice Lifecycle**: From creation to payment tracking
- **Client Relationship Management**: Detailed client profiles and history
- **Product/Service Catalog**: Reusable items with pricing and tax calculations
- **Real-time Updates**: Turbo-powered partial page updates
- **Responsive Design**: Mobile-first interface with Tailwind CSS
- **Analytics Dashboard**: Visual charts and financial insights
- **Multi-language Interface**: Full English and Spanish localization

### Implementation Highlights
- **Dynamic Form Handling**: `app/javascript/controllers/form_validation_controller.js` - Client-side validation
- **Interactive Charts**: `app/javascript/controllers/charts_controller.js` - Data visualization
- **Modal Interactions**: `app/javascript/controllers/modal_controller.js` - Enhanced UX patterns
- **Search & Filter**: `app/javascript/controllers/filter_controller.js` - Advanced data filtering
- **Auto-calculation**: `app/javascript/controllers/recalculate_controller.js` - Real-time totals

### Feature Architecture
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Registration
    Registration --> Onboarding
    Onboarding --> Dashboard
    
    Dashboard --> Clients: Manage
    Dashboard --> Invoices: Create
    Dashboard --> Items: Catalog
    
    Clients --> NewClient: Add
    Invoices --> NewInvoice: Create
    Invoices --> EditInvoice: Modify
    
    NewClient --> ClientList: Save
    NewInvoice --> InvoiceDetail: Generate
    EditInvoice --> InvoiceDetail: Update
    
    ClientList --> [*]: Exit
    InvoiceDetail --> [*]: Exit
```

### Advanced Features
- **Turbo Streams**: Real-time partial updates for seamless user experience
- **JBuilder APIs**: JSON endpoints for mobile and third-party integrations
- **Background Jobs**: `app/jobs/application_job.rb` - Asynchronous task processing
- **Testing Suite**: Comprehensive RSpec tests for models, controllers, and mailers
- **Deployment Ready**: Procfile and build scripts for cloud deployment

---

**Sources:**
- [application.rb](config/application.rb)
- [routes.rb](config/routes.rb)
- [schema.rb](db/schema.rb)
- [Gemfile](Gemfile)
- [user.rb](app/models/user.rb)
- [invoice.rb](app/models/invoice.rb)
- [client.rb](app/models/client.rb)
- [item.rb](app/models/item.rb)
- [dashboard.rb](app/models/dashboard.rb)

</blog>
